name: Content Delivery System
scenario: You're building the content delivery backend for an OTT platform (think Hotstar, Netflix India, Amazon Prime). The platform delivers different content types: Movies, Series (episodes), Podcasts, and LiveStreams. Each type delivers content differently — movies stream a file, series stream a specific episode, podcasts serve audio, livestreams redirect to a CDN URL. A single delivery engine handles all content types. When new content types are added (documentaries, shorts, sports), the delivery engine needs NO CHANGES. Pure polymorphism.
description: Build a content delivery system demonstrating the Open/Closed Principle via polymorphism: ContentItem base class, specialized subclasses, a single DeliveryEngine that processes any content type without modification.

core.description: `ContentItem` base: title, durationMinutes, contentId (static auto), isActive (boolean). deliver() prints generic message. getMetadata() returns formatted string.
`Movie extends ContentItem`: resolution (String, e.g. '4K'). Override deliver(): 'Streaming [title] in [resolution]'. Override getMetadata() using super.
`Series extends ContentItem`: seasonNumber, episodeNumber. Override deliver(): 'Streaming [title] S[season]E[episode]'. Override getMetadata().
`Podcast extends ContentItem`: hostName. Override deliver(): 'Playing podcast [title] by [host]'. Override getMetadata().
`LiveStream extends ContentItem`: cdnUrl. Override deliver(): 'Connecting to livestream [title] at [url]'. Override getMetadata().
`DeliveryEngine` class: `deliver(ContentItem item)` — checks isActive, then calls item.deliver(). NEVER modified when new content types are added.
main(): create one of each type, create DeliveryEngine, process all via engine. Also test with inactive content.

bonus.description: Add `ContentCatalog` class with ContentItem[] (max 50), addItem(), getActiveItems(), findByTitle(String) using equals.
Add `TrailerContent extends ContentItem` (a 5th type) and verify DeliveryEngine requires NO changes.
Add `totalDuration(ContentItem[] items)` that uses polymorphism to sum durations. Returns total minutes.
Add `generateReport(ContentItem[] items)` — prints type distribution: how many Movies, Series, Podcasts, LiveStreams (uses instanceof for counting).

data_to_use:
Create:
- Movie: 'RRR', 179 min, '4K'
- Series: 'Scam 1992', 60 min (per ep), season 1, episode 3
- Podcast: 'Lex Fridman #400', 120 min, host 'Lex Fridman'
- LiveStream: 'IPL Match', 240 min, 'cdn.hotstar.com/ipl-live'
- One inactive Movie for testing

Expected (core):
```
[#1] Streaming RRR in 4K
[#2] Streaming Scam 1992 S1E3
[#3] Playing podcast Lex Fridman #400 by Lex Fridman
[#4] Connecting to livestream IPL Match at cdn.hotstar.com/ipl-live
[#5] Content unavailable (inactive)
```
example_output:
Streaming RRR in 4K [179 min]
Streaming Scam 1992 S1E3 [60 min]
Playing podcast Lex Fridman #400 by Lex Fridman [120 min]
Connecting to livestream IPL Match at cdn.hotstar.com/ipl-live [240 min]
Content not available: Old Movie

criteria: Each subclass with correct fields (10 pts)
Each overrides getMetadata() using super (10 pts)
Adding bonus TrailerContent required no DeliveryEngine changes (5 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The key insight: DeliveryEngine.deliver(ContentItem item) calls item.deliver() — runtime dispatch handles everything. Each content type's deliver() is standalone. When you add a new type, DeliveryEngine literally doesn't change.
submission_instructions: Save as OTTPlatform.java. Run it. Push to GitHub. Note in your README: 'DeliveryEngine never changes as new content types are added — Open/Closed Principle via polymorphism.' This is real OTT backend architecture.
tips_for_success: Start with ContentItem and get it right before writing subclasses.
Each subclass: super() in constructor, @Override on deliver() and getMetadata().
getMetadata() in subclass: 'Title: [super.getMetadata()] | Resolution: 4K' — chain super's info.
Test: add a 5th content type (SportContent, ShortFilm, anything). Does DeliveryEngine need changes? No — that's the proof.
concepts_reinforced: Polymorphism — one method works for all subclasses
Runtime dispatch — item.deliver() calls the right override
Open/Closed Principle — engine closed for modification, open for extension
Upcasting — ContentItem[] holds Movie, Series, Podcast, LiveStream
instanceof for bonus report — acceptable use for counting types
what_youll_learn_by_doing_this:
Netflix, Hotstar, Prime — their content delivery systems ARE this architecture. MediaItem → VideoContent, AudioContent → Movie, Series, Short, Documentary. A single delivery layer routes everything. New content types plug in. The polymorphism IS the scalability.

This project PROVES the Open/Closed Principle to you empirically: add the 5th content type (the bonus), and confirm DeliveryEngine touches nothing. This feeling — 'I added a type and everything just worked' — is the PAYOFF of OOP design. Chase this feeling in your career.
